title: Q DVD BD Release News Questions
display_title: Q DVD BD Release News Questions
wikitext:
[]: Q DVD/BD Release News/Questions             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Jan 13, 2013 3:02 am As the title implies, this is a place to post any news or inquiries about the DVD/BD release of Evangelion 3.0. I guess I'll start things off with a question: Can someone explain why anime production companies wait so long to release their movies on home video? For example: The Dark Knight Rises Theater release - July 20, 2012 Home video release - December 4, 2012 About 5.5 months wait The Disappearance of Haruhi Suzumiya Theater release - February 6, 2010 Home video release - December 18, 2010 About 10.5 months wait

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 13, 2013 3:30 am This thread might answer the question.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jan 13, 2013 5:19 am The Haruhi release was set by the date on which the story itself takes place. But Japanese home video releases are typically much slower than the US practice, anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Jan 13, 2013 11:21 am Well, that does answer the question of the long wait for Funimation's release. I was more curious about why Japanese domestic releases happen much slower than those of American movies. Do you think there's a chance they might do an earlier "3.03-type" release, or will they go straight to 3.33?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Jan 14, 2013 9:55 am Someone is growing impatient after deciding to skip the camrip! About the 3.03/3.33 thing, I suppose that they re-released Jo also because 1.01 got released on DVD only, I see little reason for an "intermediate" release of Q, at least in the short-medium term (I'm still waiting for an eventual retconned version of the films a la "Director's Cut").

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Jan 14, 2013 11:34 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: Someone is growing impatient after deciding to skip the camrip!

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Jan 14, 2013 5:48 pm riffraff11235 wrote: or possibly a box set of the series, EoE and Rebuild.

[]:             Quote Post by Oenara » Mon Jan 14, 2013 6:29 pm Since there are some relevant and informative posts in the thread Reichu linked, could it be merged with this one? From that thread: svenge wrote: Here's a history of Evangelion movies (listing both their theatrical debuts and initial home video releases): Death and Rebirth 03/15/1997 theatrical debut 12/23/1998 VHS/LD release (Theatrical Edition box set with Death(True)² replacing original version of Death, also w/ EoE) Revival of Evangelion (theatrical re-release of Death(True)², Air, and Magokoro wo Kimi ni) 03/15/1998 theatrical debut 12/23/1998 VHS/LD release of Theatrical Edition box set (included "Rebirth" as well)

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Jan 14, 2013 7:03 pm Oenara wrote: Is it possible that, especially considering the substantial profit 3.0 is generating at the box office, we may have to wait not one, but two years for the Japanese BD/DVD?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Jan 14, 2013 7:06 pm riffraff11235 wrote: I sincerely hope not. One year is already a bit of a stretch.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Jan 14, 2013 8:25 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: Oh, just watch the camrip already! Unless, of course, it's really against your principles. In that case, I wish you the best.

[]:             Quote Post by Agentomega » Mon Jan 14, 2013 10:09 pm riffraff11235 wrote: The camrip is really tempting, but I'm certain that the second it ends I'll wish I had held out for better quality. I know myself way too well. Waiting it out will only make watching it all the more satisfying!

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jan 14, 2013 10:21 pm Funimation teamed up with Geneon Universal to release Black Lagoon to the US on DVD and BD recently, and they also teamed up with Warner Brothers to release Summer Wars to the States. If Anno really wants to wait out for a larger American distributor to pick up his shiny new Eva films before licensing it to a smaller one like Funimation, Funi should really consider licensing with larger distributors again for Q and FINAL. Come on, Funi. You know we both want you to have these movies faster.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Jan 14, 2013 10:25 pm Agentomega wrote: I've kind of become a quality snob since then XD

[]:             Quote Post by itreybaybayi » Wed Feb 06, 2013 1:18 am Is there any clue to when they might be releasing 3.0 in the us?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Feb 06, 2013 1:22 am Nope. You'd probably first see news here , though.

[]:             Quote Post by itreybaybayi » Wed Feb 06, 2013 1:29 am I hope its somtime thos year. So 3.0 is suppose be the last two movies in one right? The liner notes in 1.11 said so ordidthey scrap that idea?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 06, 2013 1:57 am On past form, the Japanese home video release will be this summer, then we wait about a year for the US release, and a few more months for the UK one. All plans from the original Rebuild proposal had clearly been abandoned long ago -- that suggested the whole project would take 2-3 years tops.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Wed Feb 06, 2013 3:23 am Which means will probably get a Evangelion 3.33 release some where in 2014 when it comes out in North America.

[]:             Quote Post by Chiral_Spiral » Wed Feb 06, 2013 3:51 am itreybaybayi wrote: I hope its somtime thos year. So 3.0 is suppose be the last two movies in one right?

I guess I'll start things off with a question: Can someone explain why anime production companies wait so long to release their movies on home video?
